And Notice Of Motion For Order Allowing Tardy Expert Witness Information Disclosure; And To Allow Augmentation Of Expert Witness Disclosure Information
Off Calendar Jul-11-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Dan M. Himmelheber, Esq., appearing for plaintiff & moving party: Ruth Dawson
No other appearance.

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Ruth Dawson's Motion For Order Allowing Tardy Expert Witness Information Disclosure And To Allow Augmentation Of Expert Witness Disclosure Information is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as it seeks tardy expert witness disclosure. The motion is denied as it seeks to augment its expert witness disclosure as the trial date has been continued. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses; Separate Statement
Off Calendar Aug-31-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance: Heather N. Tanner, Esq., for Plaintiff and Moving Party.
Scott D. Wiener, Esq., for Defendant and Opposing Party.
Hearing held and Tentative Ruling adopted;
Defendant's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses is moot except as the supplemental responses have not been verified. Plaintiff is ordered to verify the supplemental responses within 10 days of notice of this order. The court finds that the late served supplemental responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are imposed against Plaintiff in the amount of $350, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order.
COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
